mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, reducing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or CPIAUCSL fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through corporate earnings optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-expanding GDP/consumer spending periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect vanishes (IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost corporate margins and generate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in declining unemployment periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or UNRATE fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates supply-side disruptions, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in supply-constrained periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or INDPRO fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million units signal robust demand for construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-housing-start periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or HOUST fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A 100k monthly increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong employment growth, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-payroll growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or PAYEMS fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal inflationary pressures with tightening monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-increasing CPI/FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect vanishes (IC < 0.01).
